title: carex novae-angliae var. emmonsii
intro: Carex novae-angliae var. emmonsii, also known as Emmons' sedge, is a type of flowering plant in the sedge family (Cyperaceae). It is native to North America and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to have health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and improving wound healing, due to its bioactive compounds. Emmons' sedge can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or salves, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
